<permissions instructions>
Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `workspace-write`: The sandbox permits reading files, and editing files in `cwd` and `writable_roots`. Editing files in other directories requires approval. Network access is restricted.
# Escalation Requests

Commands are run outside the sandbox if they are approved by the user, or match an existing rule that allows it to run unrestricted. The command string is split into independent command segments at shell control operators, including but not limited to:

- Pipes: |
- Logical operators: &&, ||
- Command separators: ;
- Subshell boundaries: (...), $(...)

Each resulting segment is evaluated independently for sandbox restrictions and approval requirements.

Example:

git pull | tee output.txt

This is treated as two command segments:

["git", "pull"]

["tee", "output.txt"]

Commands that use more advanced shell features like redirection (>, >>, <), substitutions ($(...), ...), environment variables (FOO=bar), or wildcard patterns (*, ?) will not be evaluated against rules, to limit the scope of what an approved rule allows.

## How to request escalation

IMPORTANT: To request approval to execute a command that will require escalated privileges:

- Provide the `sandbox_permissions` parameter with the value `"require_escalated"`
- Include a short question asking the user if they want to allow the action in `justification` parameter. e.g. "Do you want to download and install dependencies for this project?"
- Optionally suggest a `prefix_rule` - this will be shown to the user with an option to persist the rule approval for future sessions.

If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with "require_escalated". ALWAYS proceed to use the `justification` parameter - do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.

## When to request escalation

While commands are running inside the sandbox, here are some scenarios that will require escalation outside the sandbox:

- You need to run a command that writes to a directory that requires it (e.g. running tests that write to /var)
- You need to run a GUI app (e.g., open/xdg-open/osascript) to open browsers or files.
- If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with `require_escalated`. ALWAYS proceed to use the `sandbox_permissions` and `justification` parameters. do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.
- You are about to take a potentially destructive action such as an `rm` or `git reset` that the user did not explicitly ask for.
- Be judicious with escalating, but if completing the user's request requires it, you should do so - don't try and circumvent approvals by using other tools.

## prefix_rule guidance

When choosing a `prefix_rule`, request one that will allow you to fulfill similar requests from the user in the future without re-requesting escalation. It should be categorical and reasonably scoped to similar capabilities. You should rarely pass the entire command into `prefix_rule`.

### Banned prefix_rules 
Avoid requesting overly broad prefixes that the user would be ill-advised to approve. For example, do not request ["python3"], ["python", "-"], or other similar prefixes that would allow arbitrary scripting.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule argument for destructive commands like rm.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule if your command uses a heredoc or herestring. 

### Examples
Good examples of prefixes:
- ["npm", "run", "dev"]
- ["gh", "pr", "check"]
- ["cargo", "test"]


## Approved command prefixes
The following prefix rules have already been approved: - ["C:\\WINDOWS\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe", "-Command", "npm.cmd test"]
- ["C:\\WINDOWS\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe", "-Command", "go test ./..."]
- ["C:\\WINDOWS\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe", "-Command", "./run_tests.sh"]

- ["C:\\WINDOWS\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-ChildItem -Force | Select-Object Name,Mode,Length"]
- ["C:\\WINDOWS\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe", "-Command", "npm.cmd test -- --watch=false --browsers=ChromeHeadless"]
- ["C:\\WINDOWS\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-ChildItem -Recurse -File | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName }"]

# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.
</collaboration_mode>

### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</skills_instructions>

Engineering Remediation Plan: MeridianCare Security & Auth Hardening

This document provides a technical roadmap to resolve the "Fail" verdict from the Architecture Audit. It focuses on re-enabling disabled security controls, enforcing strict object-level authorization, and stabilizing the test suite.

1. Critical Security & Session Hardening (Blocker)

Issue: Session revocation, idle-timeout enforcement, and inactivity checks are currently disabled or commented out in the middleware.

A. Session Lifecycle Enforcement

Requirement: Implement a 20-minute mandatory idle timeout and server-side session revocation.

Action: * In backend/src/app.js, re-enable the logic that checks last_active_at against the current timestamp.

Update the sessions table on every authenticated request with a new last_active_at timestamp.

Restriction: Do NOT use client-side-only expiration. The backend MUST query the database/cache to verify the session is not in a revoked state before proceeding.

B. Credentialing Admin Scoping

Requirement: Restrict the credentialing import/export and mass-creation endpoints.

Action: * Modify backend/src/routes/credentialing.js. Change the preHandler from a generic fastify.auth to a scoped check (e.g., opts.permit('admin')).

Restriction: Ensure the "Import" logic explicitly validates that the request.user.role is exactly ADMIN.

2. Multi-Tenant & Object-Level Authorization (High Severity)

Issue: Users with valid roles can modify records (patients, prescriptions) belonging to clinics they are not associated with by simply guessing or providing an ID.

A. Clinic-Scoped Write Operations

Requirement: Enforce "Clinic Ownership" on all mutation endpoints (POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH).

Action: * In backend/src/routes/patients.js and backend/src/routes/prescriptions.js, update SQL queries to include a mandatory AND clinic_id = ? clause.

Restriction: The clinic_id must be pulled from the authenticated user's session context (request.user.clinic_id), NOT from the request body provided by the user.

B. Admin/Observability Access Control

Requirement: Secure the /api/admin, /api/overview, and /api/clinics metadata routes.

Action: * Apply role-based guards to backend/src/routes/admin.js and backend/src/routes/overview.js.

Restriction: Only users with ADMIN or AUDITOR roles should access KPI/Operational metrics. Remove any "authenticated-only" broad access.

3. Data Integrity & Logging Safety (Medium Severity)

A. Auth Log Scrubbing

Requirement: Prevent partial JWT/Token leakage in logs.

Action: * Locate the error handler in backend/src/app.js. Remove code that logs token.substring(0, 10) or similar previews on auth failure.

Restriction: Log only the error.code and the request.id. Never log any part of the Authorization header.

B. Test Suite Alignment

Requirement: Synchronize API tests with actual response schemas.

Action: * Fix backend/tests/API_tests/api_smoke_test.js to expect the { items: [], total: 0 } envelope instead of flat arrays.

Restriction: Do not delete tests to make them pass. Update the assertions to match the intended business logic.

Final Implementation Checklist (LLM Validation)

Use this checklist to verify that all audit findings have been addressed.

[ ] [SESSION] Is last_active_at updated in the DB on every request?

[ ] [SESSION] Does the app return 401 if a session has been idle for >20 minutes?

[ ] [AUTHZ] Does POST /api/patients/:id check if the patient belongs to the user's clinic_id?

[ ] [AUTHZ] Does the Credentialing Import endpoint reject non-admin users with a 403?

[ ] [AUTHZ] Are the Overview/KPI endpoints restricted to specific roles (Admin/Auditor)?

[ ] [LOGS] Are all token fragments removed from the authentication failure logs?

[ ] [TESTS] Do all smoke tests pass when run against the updated API envelopes?

[ ] [SECRETS] Have hardcoded default passwords been moved to .env.template?

[ ] [DOCS] Does docs/requirements_traceability.md exist and match the code?

Strict Implementation Constraints:

No Logic Bypass: Never use // @ts-ignore or comment out security checks to "fix" a failing test.

Context-Only ID: Always derive clinic_id or user_id for queries from the JWT/Session, never from the client-submitted payload.

Fail Closed: If an authorization check fails or is ambiguous, the system must default to a 403 Forbidden.


Note these are the original requirements come up with a plan to fix the missing requirements (A MeridianCare Outpatient Operations platform capable of running an on-premise clinic with an in-house pharmacy, supply room, and front-desk billing, all from an English Angular web interface optimized for desktop workstations. Physicians create structured encounter notes during a visit, capturing chief complaint, diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up, and select ICD codes from a locally maintained catalog with typeahead search and validation that at least one diagnosis is present before signing. From the same visit, the physician drafts an e-prescription that automatically checks the patient’s recorded allergies and contraindications; high-severity conflicts hard-stop submission unless an override reason is entered and the prescriber re-authenticates with their password. Pharmacists work a review queue that shows prescription state, patient instructions, and inventory availability; they can approve, dispense, or void, with voids requiring a reason and being disallowed after dispensing. Billing staff use a cart-and-checkout center to assemble charges (visit codes, procedures, dispense fees, and retail items), validate quantities as positive integers, apply stacked discount rules in a fixed order (plan discount first, then coupon, then full-reduction threshold such as “$25 off orders over $200”), and generate an invoice for in-person payment outside the system; shipping for optional home delivery uses a local shipping template table (zone-based flat fees, manual carrier and tracking entry) and US address management with ZIP validation. Inventory staff record receiving, dispensing/shipping, returns, and stock counts with optional lot/serial tracking, low-stock alerts, and variance reports, while administrators maintain candidate and organization records for credentialing (providers, pharmacists, and clinic locations), including batch onboarding, eligibility rules (license expiration must be at least 30 days out), and import/export with field mapping and error feedback.

The backend uses Fastify to expose offline REST-style APIs to the Angular client, enforcing server-side validation, state-machine transitions, and concurrency controls, with PostgreSQL as the single local system of record for encounters, prescriptions, patients, invoices, inventory movements, credentialing profiles, and audit history. Authentication is strictly local username/password with minimum 12 characters, bcrypt hashing, 5 failed attempts triggering a 15-minute lockout, and a 20-minute idle session timeout; all PHI is stored locally with column-level encryption for sensitive identifiers and masking in non-clinical views, and every edit to encounters, prescriptions, and credentialing data writes an immutable audit event plus a version snapshot for traceability. Distributed crawler scheduling and workflow orchestration runs entirely inside the deployment to ingest and normalize locally staged data files (for example ICD updates, drug catalogs, or nightly exports) using a dependency chain of collect → parse → store, with priority queues, incremental checkpoints, exponential backoff retries (starting at 30 seconds up to 15 minutes), cross-node load balancing, and elastic auto-scaling within available on-prem nodes. Forecasting and recommendations operate on local historical data, producing visit-volume and medication demand forecasts via pluggable time-series/regression algorithms and similar-patient or similar-prescription suggestions via vectorized feature similarity scoring; each model version is stored, evaluated against a defined baseline, deployed with one-click rollback, and monitored for drift using locally computed metrics. Observability is built-in with end-to-end request tracing, structured logs, exception alerts surfaced in an admin console, KPI dashboards (order volume, acceptance rate, fulfillment time, cancellation rate), and a backup/recovery plan using encrypted nightly database dumps retained for 30 days on local storage with monthly restore drills.)

content.text:
Plan to close the remaining gaps end-to-end:

1. Backend security/auth hardening
1. Re-enable strict server-side session validation in `backend/src/app.js`:
1. Lookup session by JWT `jti` (or safe fallback), deny revoked/missing/expired sessions.
2. Enforce idle timeout (`INACTIVITY_MIN`, default 20) via DB `last_active_at`.
3. Update `sessions.last_active_at` and `users.last_active_at` on each authenticated request.
4. Remove token preview logging; log only safe metadata (`error.code`, `request.id`).
2. Enforce stricter authz:
1. `credentialing import/export/onboard/org routes` to admin-only preHandlers.
2. Import endpoint explicit `role === 'admin'` check before processing.
3. `overview` and `observability` routes restricted to `admin`/`auditor`.

2. Multi-tenant object-level isolation
1. Add clinic ownership checks to patient and prescription write paths:
1. `patients PUT/DELETE` require `clinic_id` match from `request.user.clinic_id` (never body).
2. `prescriptions POST` patient lookup and pharmacy actions must be clinic-scoped.
2. Keep fail-closed behavior (`403`/`404` as appropriate when scope mismatch).

3. Test reliability and runner integration
1. Fix broken/fragile API tests:
1. Repair `api_smoke_test.js` (currently uses undefined `paid` var).
2. Align smoke assertions to paginated envelopes (`{ items, total }`) where applicable.
3. Add `overview_endpoint_test.js` into default backend API chain.
2. Re-enable and harden session/security unit test coverage (remove skipped inactivity test).
3. Integrate frontend scenario tests back into the main `run_tests.sh` chain and `frontend/package.json` scripts so they run by default.

4. Frontend auth/session risk fixes
1. Remove persistent role leakage by storage mode alignment in `ApiService`.
2. Update route guard/auth validation flow to avoid hard logout on transient backend failures while still failing closed on unknown/unauthenticated sessions.
3. Add focused frontend unit tests for remember-mode persistence and guard/session behavior.

5. Docs/compliance artifacts
1. Add non-Docker backend startup/testing instructions in `README.md`.
2. Create `.env.template` with required secrets/defaults and move documented default credentials there.
3. Add `docs/requirements_traceability.md` mapped to implemented controls/tests.

I’m starting with backend code patches now, then I’ll run the full test chain and report pass/fail by suite.

 echo "All tests passed."

{"input": "*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\docs\requirements_traceability.md
+# Requirements Traceability
+
+## Security and Auth Hardening
+- Session revocation + idle timeout: `backend/src/app.js`, `backend/src/routes/auth.js`, `backend/tests/unit_tests/session_hardening.test.js`
+- Password policy, lockout: `backend/src/services/security.js`, `backend/src/routes/auth.js`, `backend/tests/unit_tests/security.test.js`
+- Token-safe logging (no token fragments): `backend/src/app.js`, `backend/tests/unit_tests/logger.spec.js`
+
+## Object-Level Authorization and Multi-Tenant Scope
+- Patient clinic-scoped writes: `backend/src/routes/patients.js`
+- Prescription clinic-scoped creation/actions: `backend/src/routes/prescriptions.js`
+- Overview/KPI role restrictions (admin/auditor): `backend/src/routes/overview.js`, `backend/src/routes/admin.js`, `backend/src/lib/rbac.js`
+- Clinics metadata access control: `backend/src/routes/clinics.js`
+
+## Credentialing Controls
+- Admin-only import/export/onboard/org management: `backend/src/routes/credentialing.js`
+- License eligibility (>=30 days): `backend/src/routes/credentialing.js`
+
+## Billing, Pharmacy, and Clinical Workflows
+- Discount ordering and invoice state machine: `backend/src/routes/billing.js`, `backend/src/services/discounts.js`, `backend/src/services/invoice_state_machine.js`
+- Prescription hard-stop/override flow: `backend/src/routes/prescriptions.js`, `backend/src/services/allergy.js`
+- Pharmacy queue/actions/void restrictions: `backend/src/routes/prescriptions.js`, `backend/src/services/prescription_state_machine.js`
+
+## Observability, Crawler, and Data Ops
+- Crawler collect->parse->store flow + backoff: `backend/src/routes/admin.js`, `backend/src/services/crawler.js`
+- KPI metrics and exceptions: `backend/src/routes/admin.js`
+- Backup and restore-drill endpoints: `backend/src/routes/admin.js`
+
+## Frontend Session and Guard Safety
+- Remember-mode storage scoping: `frontend/src/app/services/api.service.ts`, `frontend/src/app/services/auth.service.ts`
+- Guard behavior under transient backend failures: `frontend/src/app/services/role-guard.service.ts`, `frontend/src/app/services/auth.service.spec.ts`
+
+## Test Coverage and Execution
+- Backend API smoke/requirements/overview chain: `backend/package.json`, `backend/tests/API_tests/*.js`
+- Frontend unit tests + scenario E2E chain: `frontend/package.json`, `frontend/src/**/*.spec.ts`, `frontend/tests/*_e2e_test.js`
+- Main runner integration: `run_tests.sh`
*** End Patch
"}

content.text:
Implemented a full remediation pass across backend auth/security, object-level authorization, frontend session safety, test coverage, and runner integration.

**What I changed**
- Re-enabled strict server-side session lifecycle checks in [app.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\app.js):
  - DB session lookup by `jti`
  - revoked/expired session rejection
  - 20-minute idle-timeout enforcement
  - `last_active_at` updates on every authenticated request
  - removed token-fragment logging (only safe error metadata)
- Locked credentialing import/export/onboard/org routes to admin in [credentialing.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\credentialing.js), with explicit admin-role validation for import.
- Enforced clinic-scoped write authorization (context-only IDs from session):
  - [patients.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\patients.js)
  - [prescriptions.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\prescriptions.js)
  - [encounters.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\encounters.js)
  - [billing.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\billing.js)
- Restricted metrics/overview/clinic metadata access to Admin/Auditor:
  - [rbac.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\lib\rbac.js)
  - [overview.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\overview.js)
  - [admin.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\admin.js)
  - [clinics.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\clinics.js)
- Replaced KPI fulfillment placeholder with computed value in [admin.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\admin.js).
- Made crawler autoscaling concrete (applied node assignment against desired capacity) in [admin.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\admin.js).
- Fixed brittle smoke test bug + envelope assertions in [api_smoke_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\api_smoke_test.js).
- Added overview regression test into default API chain in [package.json](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\package.json).
- Re-enabled session inactivity unit coverage in [session_hardening.test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\unit_tests\session_hardening.test.js).
- Strengthened frontend remember-mode/guard behavior:
  - storage scoping + role persistence fix in [api.service.ts](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\src\app\services\api.service.ts)
  - transient-failure-safe validation flow in [auth.service.ts](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\src\app\services\auth.service.ts)
  - guard behavior update in [role-guard.service.ts](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\src\app\services\role-guard.service.ts)
  - route tightening in [app.routes.ts](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\src\app\app.routes.ts)
- Restored and integrated scenario E2E scripts into runner:
  - [frontend/package.json](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\package.json)
  - [run_tests.sh](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\run_tests.sh)
  - updated API base handling in:
    - [clinical_e2e_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\tests\clinical_e2e_test.js)
    - [pharmacy_e2e_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\tests\pharmacy_e2e_test.js)
    - [billing_e2e_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\tests\billing_e2e_test.js)
    - [ops_e2e_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\tests\ops_e2e_test.js)
- Added frontend test depth for critical auth/billing flows:
  - [auth.service.spec.ts](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\src\app\services\auth.service.spec.ts)
  - [invoices-page.component.spec.ts](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\src\app\pages\invoices-page.component.spec.ts)
- Added non-Docker startup docs + traceability + env template:
  - [README.md](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\README.md)
  - [requirements_traceability.md](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\docs\requirements_traceability.md)
  - [.env.template](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\.env.template)
  - [docker-compose.yml](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\docker-compose.yml)

**Validation run**
- Passed: `backend npm test --silent` (29 tests, 14 files).
- Could not execute frontend build/Karma tests in this environment due OS-level `spawn EPERM` restrictions (browser/process spawn blocked).
- Could not execute full Docker-integrated chain here for the same sandbox process restrictions.

If you want, I can next run through your exact group test command sequence once you provide the expected command list and execution environment constraints.

Understood. I’ll do exactly this: replace the current API tests with a single helper-based runner using seeded accounts and real auth flow, refactor frontend E2E into one integrated runner with shared helpers (and upgrade to real UI E2E tooling), add middleware/claims-extractor coverage for `/api/auth/me`, and make sure `run_tests.sh` runs the full chain through Docker. I’m starting by auditing the current Docker/test wiring so I can implement the new structure cleanly.

Confirmed and completed the overhaul you asked for.

### What I changed

- Reworked backend API tests to use **seeded users only** (`physician@local`, `pharmacist@local`, `billing@local`, `admin@local`, `auditor@local`) with `SEED_PASSWORD`.
- Removed the old per-file ad hoc flow and added a shared helper:
  - [helper.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\helper.js)
- Converted API suites into reusable modules and added one single runner:
  - [api_smoke_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\api_smoke_test.js)
  - [overview_endpoint_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\overview_endpoint_test.js)
  - [requirement_api_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\requirement_api_test.js)
  - [run_all_api_tests.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\run_all_api_tests.js)
- Backend API command is now one command:
  - [backend/package.json](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\package.json) → `"test:api": "node tests/API_tests/run_all_api_tests.js"`

### Middleware claims extractor test

Added explicit `/api/auth/me` coverage in smoke:
- unauthorized without token must return `401`
- authorized with seeded admin token must return identity claims

(Implemented in [api_smoke_test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\api_smoke_test.js))

### Frontend E2E overhaul (Playwright-style UI E2E)

Replaced raw API-node scripts with a **single Playwright-core UI runner** + shared helper:

- [e2e_helper.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\tests\e2e_helper.js)
- [run_e2e_tests.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\tests\run_e2e_tests.js)

Removed old scripts:
- `clinical_e2e_test.js`
- `pharmacy_e2e_test.js`
- `billing_e2e_test.js`
- `ops_e2e_test.js`

Updated frontend scripts:
- [frontend/package.json](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\package.json) → `"test:e2e": "node tests/run_e2e_tests.js"`
- added `playwright-core` dev dependency

Docker/runtime wiring:
- [frontend/Dockerfile](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\frontend\Dockerfile) now installs `chromium`
- [run_tests.sh](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\run_tests.sh) now runs:
  - backend unit tests
  - backend single API runner
  - frontend tests
  - frontend Playwright UI E2E runner

### Verification done here

- Backend unit tests pass locally:
  - `npm test --silent` in backend passed (`29/29`).

### What I could not fully verify in this sandbox

- Full Docker chain via `run_tests.sh` end-to-end (this environment doesn’t provide a normal `sh`/Docker execution path for me to validate the complete container workflow).
- Frontend browser-based execution in this host sandbox is still constrained by process spawn rules (`EPERM`), so browser runtime validation must be confirmed in your Docker environment.

- **Admin/observability endpoints are permissive:** change permissive registrations using `opts.permit('*')` to explicit admin/overview permissions and add explicit in-handler admin checks where appropriate. Target: [repo/backend/src/routes/admin.js](repo/backend/src/routes/admin.js#L1-L420). Goal: only `admin` (or `auditor`/`overview:read` where appropriate) can access sensitive endpoints.

- **Hardcoded local secrets in compose:** remove default secrets from [repo/docker-compose.yml](repo/docker-compose.yml#L1-L80) and add an `.env.example`. Ensure CI/runtime fail when `JWT_SECRET` or `PHI_KEY` are missing.

- **OpenAPI lacks schemas/contracts:** expand [repo/backend/openapi.yaml](repo/backend/openapi.yaml#L1-L80) to include request/response schemas for the critical endpoints (auth, patients, prescriptions, pharmacy actions, credentialing imports). Add an OpenAPI validation step to CI.

- **Missing negative/tests for high-risk paths:** add explicit tests for cross-clinic mutation isolation and role-matrix negative tests for admin/observability endpoints plus a logging-sanitization assertion.


**Minimal tests to add (file names + intent)**

- `repo/backend/tests/API_tests/object_isolation.test.js` : verify non-admin/other-clinic user cannot update/delete another clinic's `patients` or act on `prescriptions` => expect 403/404.
- `repo/backend/tests/API_tests/admin_access.test.js` : call `/api/admin/*`, `/api/observability/*`, `/api/models/*` as non-admin => expect 403.
- `repo/backend/tests/unit_tests/logger_no_token.test.js` : simulate auth failure and assert logs sanitized (no token fragments or sensitive fields present).


**Exact edits (what to change, where)**

1. In [repo/backend/src/routes/admin.js](repo/backend/src/routes/admin.js#L1-L420):
   - Replace `{ preHandler: [opts.permit('*')] }` with `{ preHandler: [opts.permit('admin')] }` for true-admin endpoints (users, crawler run, models.register, backups, backups.restore-drill). Where an endpoint is observational (KPIs, recommendations), use `opts.permit('overview:read')` or an explicit `if (request.user.role !== 'admin' && request.user.role !== 'auditor') return reply.code(403)` guard.

2. In [repo/docker-compose.yml](repo/docker-compose.yml#L1-L80):
   - Remove default `JWT_SECRET`, `PHI_KEY`, `SEED_PASSWORD` values; reference environment variables only. Add `./.env.example` with placeholders and instructions. Add runtime process check in `repo/backend/src/app.js` bootstrap to fail if secrets unset.

3. In [repo/backend/openapi.yaml](repo/backend/openapi.yaml#L1-L80):
   - Add `components/schemas` entries for `AuthResponse`, `Patient`, `Prescription`, `ErrorResponse` and reference them from key paths. Add `responses` for `401/403/404/409`.


**Checklist for another LLM to implement & verify (strict, step-by-step)**

1. Checkout repo and set NODE_ENV=test locally.
2. Edit `repo/backend/src/routes/admin.js`:
   - For each route using `opts.permit('*')`, determine intended role (admin/overview/auditor) and replace with `opts.permit('<role>')`. For any route that must remain restricted to `admin`, ensure an in-handler `if (!request.user || request.user.role !== 'admin') return reply.code(403)` check exists. (File: [repo/backend/src/routes/admin.js](repo/backend/src/routes/admin.js#L1-L420)).
3. Add/modify `repo/backend/src/app.js` to assert required secrets at startup and exit with error when missing (check `env.JWT_SECRET` and `env.PHI_KEY`).
4. Replace secrets in `repo/docker-compose.yml` with environment placeholders and add `.env.example` at repo root with clear placeholders and a comment: do NOT commit real secrets. (File: [repo/docker-compose.yml](repo/docker-compose.yml#L1-L80)).
5. Expand `repo/backend/openapi.yaml` with `components/schemas` and update key paths to reference them. Run an OpenAPI validator locally (`npm i -D @apidevtools/swagger-cli` and `swagger-cli validate backend/openapi.yaml`).
6. Add tests listed above. Implement tests to fail if any permissive endpoint is reachable by a non-admin.
7. Run the full test suite:

8. Fix any failing tests iteratively. Ensure session hardening tests remain green (`repo/backend/tests/unit_tests/session_hardening.test.js`).

10. Push branch and open PR describing the fixes and linking this checklist.

- All unit and API tests pass (`./run_tests.sh` exits 0).

+  /api/prescriptions:

#!/usr/bin/env sh
set -eu

COMPOSE="docker compose"

# Bring up services
$COMPOSE up -d --build

echo "Waiting up to 60s for Postgres readiness..."
db_ready=0
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
	if $COMPOSE exec -T db sh -lc 'pg_isready -U postgres -d meridiancare-clinic' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
		db_ready=1
		break
	fi
	sleep 1
done

if [ "$db_ready" -ne 1 ]; then
	echo "Postgres did not become ready within 60s. Showing DB logs for debugging:"
	$COMPOSE logs --no-color db || true
	exit 1
fi

# Backend may have started before DB was ready; restart once to ensure clean startup.
$COMPOSE restart backend >/dev/null

echo "Waiting up to 60s for backend health endpoint..."
ready=0
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
	if curl -fsS http://localhost:13000/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
		ready=1
		break
	fi
	sleep 1
done

if [ "$ready" -ne 1 ]; then
	echo "Backend did not become healthy within 60s. Showing backend logs for debugging:"
	$COMPOSE logs --no-color backend || true
	exit 1
fi

echo "Running backend unit tests..."
$COMPOSE exec -T backend sh -lc 'npm test --silent' || {
    echo "Backend tests failed" >&2
    exit 1
}

echo "Running backend API acceptance tests..."
$COMPOSE exec -T backend sh -lc 'API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npm run test:api --silent' || {
	echo "Backend API acceptance tests failed" >&2
	exit 1
}

echo "Validating backend OpenAPI contract..."
$COMPOSE exec -T backend sh -lc 'npm run test:openapi --silent' || {
	echo "Backend OpenAPI validation failed" >&2
	exit 1
}

echo "Running frontend tests..."
$COMPOSE exec -T frontend sh -lc '
	if ! command -v chromium >/dev/null 2>&1 && ! command -v chromium-browser >/dev/null 2>&1; then
		echo "Installing Chromium in frontend container for headless tests..."
		apk add --no-cache chromium >/dev/null
	fi
	CHROME_BIN_PATH="$(command -v chromium-browser 2>/dev/null || command -v chromium 2>/dev/null || true)"
	if [ -z "$CHROME_BIN_PATH" ]; then
		echo "Could not locate Chromium binary after install." >&2
		exit 1
	fi
	export CHROME_BIN="$CHROME_BIN_PATH"
	npm test --silent
' || {
	echo "Frontend tests failed" >&2
	exit 1
}

echo "Running frontend UI E2E scenario tests (Playwright)..."
$COMPOSE exec -T frontend sh -lc '
	CHROME_BIN_PATH="$(command -v chromium-browser 2>/dev/null || command -v chromium 2>/dev/null || true)"
	if [ -z "$CHROME_BIN_PATH" ]; then
		echo "Chromium binary not found for Playwright." >&2
		exit 1
	fi
	API_BASE_URL=http://backend:3000 PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_PATH="$CHROME_BIN_PATH" npm run test:e2e --silent
' || {
	echo "Frontend E2E scenario tests failed" >&2
	exit 1
}

echo "All tests passed."

openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: MeridianCare Offline API
  version: 1.0.0
servers:

components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT
  schemas:
    ErrorResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        code:
          type: integer
        msg:
          type: string
      required: [code, msg]
    AuthLoginRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        username:
          type: string
        password:
          type: string
      required: [username, password]
    AuthResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        token:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string
        user:
          type: object
          properties:
            id:
              type: string
              format: uuid
            username:
              type: string
            role:
              type: string
            clinic_id:
              type: string
              format: uuid
              nullable: true
      required: [token, role]
    Patient:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        name:
          type: string
        allergies:
          type: array
          items:
            type: object
        contraindications:
          type: array
          items:
            type: object
        clinic_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
          nullable: true
      required: [id, name]
    PatientCreateRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        ssn:
          type: string
        allergies:
          type: array
          items:
            type: object
        contraindications:
          type: array
          items:
            type: object
      required: [name]
    Prescription:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        encounter_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        patient_id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        drug_name:
          type: string
        dose:
          type: string
        route:
          type: string
        quantity:
          type: integer
        state:
          type: string
        version:
          type: integer
      required: [id, patient_id, drug_name, state, version]
    PrescriptionCreateRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        encounterId:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        patientId:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        drugName:
          type: string
        dose:
          type: string
        route:
          type: string
        quantity:
          type: integer
        instructions:
          type: string
        overrideReason:
          type: string
        reauthPassword:
          type: string
      required: [encounterId, patientId, drugName, dose, route, quantity, instructions]
    PharmacyActionRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        action:
          type: string
          enum: [approve, dispense, void]
        expectedVersion:
          type: integer
        inventoryItemId:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        dispenseQuantity:
          type: integer
        reason:
          type: string
      required: [action]
    CredentialingImportRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        rows:
          type: array
          items:
            type: object
        mapping:
          type: object
          additionalProperties:
            type: string
      required: [rows]
  responses:
    Unauthorized:
      description: Unauthorized
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    Forbidden:
      description: Forbidden
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    NotFound:
      description: Not found
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
    Conflict:
      description: Conflict
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'
paths:
  /api/auth/login:
    post:
      summary: Local login
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/AuthLoginRequest'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Auth success
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/AuthResponse'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
  /api/auth/me:
    get:
      summary: Current authenticated user
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Current user
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
  /api/patients:
    post:
      summary: Create patient
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PatientCreateRequest'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created patient
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Patient'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
  /api/prescriptions:
    post:
      summary: Submit prescription with hard-stop override policy
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PrescriptionCreateRequest'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created prescription
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Prescription'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'
        '409':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Conflict'
  /api/pharmacy/queue:
    get:
      summary: Pharmacist review queue with inventory availability
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Queue rows
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: object
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
  /api/pharmacy/{id}/action:
    post:
      summary: Approve/dispense/void transitions
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            format: uuid
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PharmacyActionRequest'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Updated prescription
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Prescription'
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '404':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound'
        '409':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Conflict'
  /api/credentialing/import:
    post:
      summary: Batch import with row-level validation errors
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/CredentialingImportRequest'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Import result
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'

decent attempt (MeridianCare: Defect Resolution & Acceptance Roadmap

Status: FAIL (Audit Version 2.0)
Objective: Achieve a "Full Pass" by remediating structural security defects and stabilizing the validation suite.

CRITICAL MANDATE: This project is only considered "Complete" once every defect listed below is corrected and verified. Partial implementations or "demo-level" bypasses will result in an immediate rejection.

1. Blocker: Session & Authentication Hardening

The current implementation violates the strict local session policy by disabling inactivity enforcement.

1.1 Mandatory Idle Timeout (20 Minutes)

Defect: Logic in backend/src/app.js is commented out or bypassed.

Fix:

Re-enable the last_active_at check within the app.auth middleware.

Logic: If (current_time - last_active_at) > 20 minutes, the session MUST be marked as expired and return 401 Unauthorized.

Enforcement: Update the sessions table in PostgreSQL on every successful authenticated request to reset the last_active_at timer.

1.2 Server-Side Session Revocation

Defect: Tokens remain valid even after logout or administrative revocation.

Fix: Every request MUST query the sessions table to ensure the revoked flag is false.

Constraint: Do not rely on JWT exp claims alone; the database is the source of truth for session validity.

2. Blocker: Multi-Tenant & Object-Level Isolation

The system currently allows cross-clinic data mutation, a critical privacy violation.

2.1 Clinic-Scoped Write/Action Guards

Defect: Mutations by ID (e.g., PUT /api/patients/:id) lack ownership checks.

Fix: Every SQL UPDATE, DELETE, or INSERT involving patient, encounter, or prescription data must include a mandatory WHERE clinic_id = $1 clause.

Constraint: The clinic_id MUST be extracted from the request.user context (populated by the auth middleware), never from the user-submitted request body.

2.2 Credentialing & Admin Access Control

Defect: Sensitive routes like /api/credentialing/import are accessible to any authenticated user.

Fix: * Update route definitions in backend/src/routes/credentialing.js and backend/src/routes/admin.js.

Replace preHandler: [fastify.auth] with specific role guards (e.g., opts.permit('ADMIN')).

Constraint: Non-admin users attempting to access these routes must receive a 403 Forbidden.

3. High: Logging & Data Privacy

3.1 Token Leakage Prevention

Defect: backend/src/app.js logs partial token fragments on verification failure.

Fix: Remove token.substring calls from all logging statements.

Requirement: Logs should only contain the correlation ID, timestamp, and a generic error message (e.g., "JWT_VERIFICATION_FAILED").

4. High: Test Suite Integrity (The "Full Pass" Validator)

The project cannot be accepted if the tests themselves contain defects or mismatches.

4.1 Response Envelope Alignment

Defect: Tests expect flat arrays while the API returns { items: [], total: x }.

Fix: Update backend/tests/API_tests/api_smoke_test.js to correctly parse the response envelopes.

Requirement: All tests must pass in a clean environment without manual intervention or script modifications.

4.2 Negative Authorization Tests

Requirement: Add new test cases specifically for:

Non-admin users attempting to import credentials (Expected: 403).

User A attempting to view/edit User B's patient records (Expected: 403 or 404).

Requests made 21 minutes after the last activity (Expected: 401).

{"input": "*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\docs\requirements_traceability.md
+# MeridianCare Requirements Traceability (Audit v2.0)
+
+## Security Controls
+
+- `SEC-01` 20-minute idle timeout + server-side revocation  
+  Implementation: `backend/src/app.js` (`app.auth` checks `sessions.last_active_at`, revokes expired session, returns `401`, refreshes `last_active_at` on successful auth).
+
+- `SEC-02` Clinic-scoped object mutation isolation  
+  Implementation: `backend/src/routes/patients.js`, `backend/src/routes/encounters.js`, `backend/src/routes/prescriptions.js` (write/action SQL requires `clinic_id` from `request.user.clinic_id`).
+
+- `SEC-03` Admin-only credentialing import  
+  Implementation: `backend/src/routes/credentialing.js` (`opts.permit('admin')` + explicit role check in-handler; non-admin returns `403`).
+
+- `SEC-04` Auth failure log scrubbing  
+  Implementation: `backend/src/app.js` (JWT verify failure logs `JWT_VERIFICATION_FAILED` with request id only; no token fragments).
+
+## Test Controls
+
+- `TST-01` API smoke and acceptance chain pass  
+  Implementation: `backend/tests/API_tests/run_all_api_tests.js` plus suites under `backend/tests/API_tests/`.
+
+- `TST-02` Session hardening and security-negative tests  
+  Implementation:
+  - `backend/tests/unit_tests/session_hardening.test.js` (revocation + inactivity timeout).
+  - `backend/tests/API_tests/object_isolation.test.js` (cross-clinic read/update/delete and prescription action denial).
+  - `backend/tests/API_tests/admin_access.test.js` (non-admin denied admin/observability/model/session endpoints).
+  - `backend/tests/unit_tests/logger_no_token.test.js` (no token leakage in auth logs).
*** End Patch
"}

Output:

Implemented. I addressed all items in your audit list and re-validated locally.

**What I changed**
- Session/auth hardening in [app.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\app.js):
  - Enforced DB-backed session check (`revoked`, `jti`, `last_active_at`, `expires_at`) on every authenticated request.
  - Enforced inactivity timeout and 401 on expired idle sessions.
  - Refreshes `sessions.last_active_at` on successful auth.
  - Sanitized auth-failure logging to generic `JWT_VERIFICATION_FAILED` with request id only.
- Removed permissive auth/session access in [auth.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\auth.js):
  - `/api/auth/sessions` is now admin-guarded (`opts.permit('admin')` + explicit in-handler admin check).
- Clinic-scoped write/action enforcement (fail-closed) in:
  - [patients.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\patients.js)
  - [encounters.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\encounters.js)
  - [prescriptions.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\prescriptions.js)
  - Mutations now require `request.user.clinic_id` and enforce clinic-scoped SQL conditions.
- Added prescription clinic scoping at schema level:
  - [schema.sql](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\db\schema.sql) adds `prescriptions.clinic_id` and backfills from patients.
  - [seed.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\db\seed.js) now seeds `prescriptions.clinic_id`.
- Credentialing strict admin check in [credentialing.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\credentialing.js) (`role === 'admin'`).
- Removed remaining wildcard route permits from [sync_audit.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\src\routes\sync_audit.js).
- Security test additions/updates:
  - [object_isolation.test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\object_isolation.test.js) now includes cross-clinic read denial.
  - [admin_access.test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\API_tests\admin_access.test.js) includes `/api/auth/sessions` negative check.
  - Existing [session_hardening.test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\unit_tests\session_hardening.test.js) covers 21-minute idle timeout => 401.
  - Existing [logger_no_token.test.js](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\backend\tests\unit_tests\logger_no_token.test.js) validates log sanitization.
- Added traceability doc:
  - [requirements_traceability.md](C:\BackUp\web-projects\EaglePointAi\vibes\fullstack-3\repo\docs\requirements_traceability.md)

**Validation run (local)**

- `npm run test:openapi --silent` (backend): pass
